message:
feat(core,planner): widen ANALYZE cache invalidation, fix correlated subquery table-ref collection, and fix NE selectivity estimate

connection.rs: replace bare planner_directive_cache.clear() in the
ANALYZE path with clear_compilation_reuse_caches(), which flushes the
prepared and compiled bytecode caches as well.  Stale query plans
embedded in those caches embed pre-ANALYZE statistics and would
silently produce suboptimal plans until evicted.  A new regression test
(test_analyze_clears_compiled_and_prepared_reuse_caches) verifies that
all three caches are empty after ANALYZE.  Also adds prepared_cache_len
test helper to mirror the existing compiled/directive helpers.

planner/lib.rs: extend collect_table_refs to recurse into correlated
subquery bodies (Exists and Subquery variants).  Previously the WHERE
clause and select list of a subquery were silently skipped, so outer
table references inside correlated subqueries were invisible to the
planner, causing incorrect join-ordering and plan-reuse decisions.

planner/stats.rs: fix the NE (not-equal) row-count estimate.  The old
code called estimate_selectivity(Eq) and multiplied by non_null_count,
but estimate_selectivity returns a fraction relative to table_row_count,
not non_null_count — producing a double-normalised and therefore too-low
estimate.  The fix computes the absolute Eq match count inline
(histogram or NDV-based) and subtracts directly from non_null_count,
clamped to 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
